goal: Classify every reusable primitive family shared by the marketing and admin React surfaces, extract only compatible semantic cores into @catch/web-ui, and keep usability, performance, organization, and coverage follow-ups explicit until they are completed or owner-blocked.

referenceImplementation.pattern: The package owns native semantics and accessibility state; website/admin adapters own density, visual class names, content, and domain mapping.
acceptance: Website ChoiceChip and ChoiceCard expose aria-pressed through the package control.
Admin SegmentedControl exposes a labelled group and pressed state through the same package control.
Both surface APIs and visual class contracts remain stable.
Package unit tests, Storybook state coverage, lexicon links, and visual baselines cover the shared behavior.

candidates.rationale: Pure behavior with identical semantics and no surface styling.
The package owns span/div element selection while each surface owns its label styling.
Native checkbox type ownership is compatible; label layout and controlled-value adapters remain surface-owned.
Both surfaces represent one-of-many choices but currently omit consistent pressed/group semantics.
The lexicon already defines compatible variant, size, loading, icon, disabled, and full-width states; surface density can remain adapter-owned.
Native control semantics are reusable, but website uncontrolled/id-based fields and admin controlled/data-option fields need stable surface adapters.
The Flutter contract supplies the missing shared title, message, icon, action, surface, and stacked/inline anatomy; surface-specific variants should map into that core.
Both own the same wrapper/table anatomy; extraction should add a consistent accessible-name contract without erasing compact/workbench styling.
The common neutral/success/warning/danger tone model is reusable after domain-specific tones are mapped by adapters.
Marketing product/claim panels and admin operational panels have different containment semantics; sharing the current wrappers would create variant sprawl.
Marketing metrics are sparse proof points while admin metrics carry operational tone and dense supporting evidence.

improvements.summary: Add group and aria-pressed semantics to website choice controls and admin segmented controls.
evidence: website ChoiceChip and ChoiceCard render selection only through class names.
admin SegmentedControl renders a labelled div but no group role or aria-pressed state.

improvements.summary: Give @catch/web-ui its own unit/typecheck loop and make Storybook states truthful.
evidence: The package currently has no scripts or unit tests.
Its registry claims span/div/surface-class and unchecked/checked/disabled states, while each current story renders only one state.

improvements.summary: Establish a token-backed keyboard focus contract across both React surfaces and restore focus-within styling for admin search/title controls.
evidence: Shared web CSS has no explicit focus-visible contract.
Admin search and marketing title inputs set outline: 0 without an equivalent visible wrapper focus state.

improvements.summary: Give every shared data table a stable accessible name and preserve responsive scroll framing.
evidence: Website DataTable requires ariaLabel.
Admin DataTable currently renders an unnamed table.

improvements.summary: Split the 4,129-line OrganizerIntakeScreen into feature-owned panels without changing the route/workspace boundary.
evidence: The route/workspace entry is now 629 lines; readiness, evidence, discovery, and event workflows live in four private modules no larger than 1,139 lines.
Lower-case module APIs preserve the admin feature-export contract and full typecheck/scanner loop.

improvements.summary: Split the 3,066-line MarketingOpsScreen into workflow panels and keep controller/render/export boundaries explicit.
evidence: MarketingOpsScreen is now a 340-line route/workspace entry with four feature-private composer, library, workflow, and preview modules.
The controller API and rendered composition remain unchanged.

improvements.summary: Split the 1,868-line EventPublishingScreen after the two larger admin reference extractions establish the panel pattern.
evidence: EventPublishingScreen is now a 197-line route/workspace entry with four private directory, supply, readiness, and editor modules.
The same lower-case private-module pattern passes feature-export and component-governance gates.

improvements.summary: Split the remaining 1,343-line OrganizerPublishingScreen outlier and ratchet admin feature UI module size.
evidence: OrganizerPublishingScreen is now 163 lines with 421-line directory and 980-line detail private modules.
The enforced budget caps route/workspace files at 900 lines and private feature modules at 1,200 lines.

improvements.summary: Add behavior tests for remaining admin query/mutation controllers, prioritizing safety, organizer publishing, event publishing, and access decisions.
evidence: The hardening tranche raised admin to 8 test files, but most feature controllers still had no colocated behavior suite.
All 14 admin feature controllers now have named behavior suites; the admin unit loop passes 22 files and 49 tests with aggregate line coverage at 48.68%.
The controller target manifest promotes every admin controller to required and rejects any missing or unclassified suite.

improvements.summary: Add targeted tables for low-coverage organizer/event publishing validation and filtering helpers without using aggregate percentage gates.
evidence: Event helper coverage exercises time, capacity, launch-market, search-index, and external-review filters.
Organizer helper coverage exercises route/search debt filters and explicit blocker/diff counters.

improvements.summary: Extend website controller tests to claim and listing-review mutations plus route-state failure cases.
Add behavior tests for the remaining claim-flow, host-listing, and organizer-directory route controllers.
Keep an accepted public review visible while the eventually consistent listing query catches up.
Reduce oversized admin Storybook route chunks without weakening route-level review coverage.
evidence: The current admin Storybook build warns that AdminRoutes.stories is approximately 1.23 MB minified and the preview runtime is approximately 1.08 MB.
The current marketing Storybook preview runtime is approximately 1.11 MB minified.
Named admin feature/data chunks reduced AdminRoutes.stories from approximately 1.14 MB to 37 KB without removing any registry-ready story.
The checked budget caps AdminRoutes at 50 KB and every Catch-authored feature/story chunk at 510 KB.

improvements.summary: Reduce the admin production entry chunk and verify route-level lazy boundaries without hiding size warnings.
evidence: The production admin entry fell from approximately 696 KB to 436 KB after the Overview controller moved behind its lazy route boundary.
The checked budget caps the entry at 460 KB and the largest async chunk at 505 KB.

improvements.summary: Remove the Overview-only time range and refresh controls from every admin topbar and colocate refresh with the Overview analytics filters.
Give structural eyebrow labels their own catch.ui_label lexicon family instead of mapping them to catch.badge.
evidence: CatchSectionLabel, website UiLabel, AdminEyebrow, and web-ui UiLabel now share catch.ui_label.
StatusBadge, StatusChip, and BadgeControl remain mapped to catch.badge, preserving status versus hierarchy semantics.

improvements.summary: Split the combined website choice-controls registry/story entry so terse chips map to catch.chip and explanatory cards map to catch.option_card independently.
Make both React workflows trigger on shared web UI source/registry changes and run the package unit/typecheck loop.
Default reusable buttons to type=button and expose one disabled, aria-busy loading contract through both surface adapters.
Normalize invalid/described-by semantics for text, select, and textarea controls and require website label ids at compile time.
Realign the canonical organizer title-template assertion with the current events-and-reviews metadata source.
Repair stale sunset, reverse-rule, role, stage, doc-anchor, and executable-check metadata reported by the enforcement-integrity meta-gate.
Continue classifying novel repeated shell families and add scanner coverage as each tracker item is implemented.
evidence: The generated governed-family registry matches the checked-in snapshot exactly.
Primitive, class-name boundary, component-governance, and cross-surface adoption scanners all pass their non-vacuous self-tests and repository checks.
